title: geanthus longifolius
intro: Geanthus longifolius, also known as the Indian snakeroot or Chota Chandan, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. Geanthus longifolius can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
